session_id: ANL-2026-04-25-l2规则使命宣言业务伤疤融入评判
topic: 评判 L2 规则 [MISSION_STATEMENT] 与 [BUSINESS_LOGIC_CHUNKS] 应如何融入 Fabric-v2

relevant_files.summary: Current planning context already makes L0/L2 required and L1 AI-selectable. It creates selection_token with required_stable_ids and ai_selectable_stable_ids.

Current section delivery supports a whitelist of MANDATORY_INJECTION and CONTEXT_INFO, parses Markdown section headings, merges required L0/L2 with selected L1, and appends rule_selection audit events.
Registry schema has RuleDescription and AgentsMetaNode fields, but no mission statement schema, business chunk schema, or anchor metadata.
Pre-commit currently scopes checks via agents.meta.json and runs sync-meta, human-lint, and ledger-append. It has no code-anchor or business chunk drift check.

The repository already has a parallel script-level knowledge layer describing script purpose, boundaries, side effects, and anchors. It is useful precedent for MISSION_STATEMENT, but it intentionally does not extend agents.meta.json.
Current repository root is not a complete Fabric-managed target project; root .fabric only contains audit.jsonl. Analysis should use tool code and fixtures rather than assuming active root registry.
relevant_files.evidence: document section '当前项目 Registry 状态'

patterns.description: fab_plan_context returns a neutral description index and selection token; fab_get_rule_sections later returns selected structured sections.
L2 rules whose scope_glob matches target path are required and cannot be skipped by AI selection.
Only whitelisted Markdown section names are currently returned. New sections require type, parser, service, tool schema, and tests.
Commit-time protection currently lives in pre-commit plus sync-meta/human-lint/ledger-append; anchor deletion warnings belong in this family.

key_findings.finding: MISSION_STATEMENT fits naturally as a new L2 rule section, not as agents.meta node metadata.
key_findings.why: Path-triggered L2 selection already exists in plan-context; section injection already exists in rule-sections.

key_findings.finding: BUSINESS_LOGIC_CHUNKS needs both a rule section and a separate anchor lifecycle check.
key_findings.why: Section delivery can inject the scar text, but cannot detect if a code comment anchor was deleted during a commit.

key_findings.finding: Dynamic pruning should start as diagnostics/archive, not silent deletion from L2 Markdown.
key_findings.why: Historical scars are safety data. Automatic deletion would make the system lose exactly the context it is meant to preserve.

key_findings.finding: Semantic merge should be implemented first as stable-ID upsert semantics, with LLM/semantic merge deferred.
key_findings.why: The system already standardizes stable_id and audit behavior. ID-based replacement is deterministic and testable.

constraints: No source code was modified during this analysis.
Current root .fabric registry is incomplete, so implementation should be tested with fixtures and initialized project examples.
Business chunk governance must not expose full rule content through plan_context; full content belongs in fab_get_rule_sections.
